name: Pueblo Obejo — circular from Pozoblanco
region_intro: Road cycling around Pozoblanco offers diverse landscapes within the Valle de Los Pedroches and the Sierra Morena mountain range in northern Andalusia. The region features rolling hills, dense forests, and winding roads with good asphalt, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Characterized by its "dehesa" landscape of extensive holm oak groves and olive plantations, the area includes elevations up to 1,000 meters. Cyclists can expect routes through picturesque villages and past serene reservoirs, with options ranging from moderate to challenging ascents. Best road cycling routes around Pozoblanco The most popular road cycling route is Villaharta, Espiel y Alcaracejos — circular desde Pozoblanco, a 57.9 miles (93.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 31 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de La Chimorra and Villaharta Loop from Pozoblanco, a difficult 54.9 miles (88.4 km) path. This route offers mountain scenery and challenging climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Route of the Seven Villas of Los Pedroches, a 29.7 miles (47.8 km) trail leading through picturesque villages and dehesa landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Pozoblanco is defined by the rolling hills of Sierra Morena, the dehesa landscape of Valle de Los Pedroches, and winding country roads. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pozoblanco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling around Pozoblanco?
A: Road cycling around Pozoblanco offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll experience the rolling hills and dense forests of the Sierra Morena mountain range, along with the unique 'dehesa' landscape of the Valle de Los Pedroches, characterized by extensive holm oak groves and picturesque olive plantations. Many routes also feature serene reservoirs like the Yeguas and Pintado, and you'll encounter winding country roads with good asphalt.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pozoblanco?
A: Yes, Pozoblanco offers routes for various skill levels. While many routes feature challenging ascents, there are 7 routes classified as easy, providing gentler options for those new to road cycling or looking for a more relaxed ride. In total, there are 34 routes to choose from.

Q: Which routes offer a challenging experience for advanced road cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Pozoblanco has 9 difficult routes. Notable options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1335216375/villaharta-espiel-y-alcaracejos-circular-desde-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaharta, Espiel y Alcaracejos — circular desde Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt;, a 93.3 km trail with significant elevation gain, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010621775/puerto-de-la-chimorra-and-villaharta-loop-from-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de La Chimorra and Villaharta Loop from Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt;, an 88.4 km path known for its mountain scenery and demanding climbs.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Pozoblanco?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Pozoblanco are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1335216375/villaharta-espiel-y-alcaracejos-circular-desde-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaharta, Espiel y Alcaracejos — circular desde Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005492422/pueblo-obejo-circular-from-pozoblanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pueblo Obejo — circular from Pozoblanco&lt;/a&gt; are excellent circular options.

Q: What are some interesting villages to visit along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is dotted with charming villages that are often integrated into cycling routes. You can explore places like Dos Torres, known for its traditional architecture, or Pedroche, Villaharta, Espiel, Alcaracejos, Añora, Villanueva de Córdoba, Cardeña, and Torrecampo, all offering a glimpse into local culture and history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010625063/route-of-the-seven-villas-of-los-pedroches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Seven Villas of Los Pedroches&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights several of these picturesque settlements.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pozoblanco?
A: Pozoblanco is particularly attractive for road cyclists during the winter months due to its pleasant climate, offering an escape from colder weather elsewhere. Spring and autumn also provide ideal conditions with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be quite hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Cyclists can encounter several significant landmarks. The emblematic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4288368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgen de Luna&lt;/a&gt; is a cultural and religious site near Pozoblanco. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malos Pasos Spring&lt;/a&gt; near Villaharta, known for its mineral waters. Many routes also lead through historic town centers like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaracejos Town Center&lt;/a&gt; or past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4667553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor De la Villa de Dos Torres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of elevation changes should I expect on road cycling routes around Pozoblanco?
A: The road cycling routes around Pozoblanco feature varied elevation changes, ranging from gentle undulations to significant climbs. The region is part of the Sierra Morena mountain range, with elevations reaching up to 1,000 meters. You'll find routes with challenging ascents, such as those that traverse mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de La Chimorra&lt;/a&gt;, as well as flatter sections through the Valle de Los Pedroches.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer particularly scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes in the Sierra Morena provide magnificent views, especially those on winding roads like the A-420, which offers vistas of the Guadalquivir valley. Routes that climb to higher elevations or pass by reservoirs, such as the Yeguas or Pintado, will also offer stunning panoramic views of the dehesa landscape and dense forests.

Q: What makes the road cycling experience in Pozoblanco unique?
A: The road cycling experience in Pozoblanco is unique due to its combination of the vast, well-preserved Mediterranean forest of Sierra Morena and the distinctive 'dehesa' landscape of the Valle de Los Pedroches. The region offers tranquil country roads, diverse terrain from rolling hills to challenging mountain passes, and the opportunity to cycle through charming, traditional villages, all under a generally pleasant climate, especially in winter.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in total around Pozoblanco?
A: There are 34 road cycling routes available around Pozoblanco on komoot, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include 7 easy routes, 18 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through olive groves or cork tree plantations?
A: Yes, the landscape around Pozoblanco is characterized by vast olive groves and cork tree plantations, particularly within the Valle de Los Pedroches. Many routes will take you through these picturesque, tree-filled pastures, offering a serene and authentic rural cycling experience.
